title: Puerto Banus Boat Rental for Sunset Cruise: Costs and Tips 2026
html:
Sunset cruising on the Mediterranean from the glamorous marina of Puerto Banus is a bucket-list experience. Private boat hire for a golden-hour trip typically ranges from €350 for a two-hour cruise on an 8-metre boat to over €2,000 for a crewed superyacht. BoatHire24 connects you directly with verified skippers and yacht charter Puerto Banus listings, so you can compare prices, photos and availability in one place.

Where to set off in Puerto Banus?

The main departure point is Puerto Banus Marina itself, with its famous 915-berth harbour lined with designer boutiques and restaurants. From here you can head west towards the exclusive residential cluster of La Zagaleta or east along the Costa del Sol past the Marbella Club Hotel. Other popular departure points include the nearby Cabopino Marina, the fishing port of Caleta de Velez, and the picturesque cove of Calahonda. Many sunset cruises also stop in the Bay of Gibraltar if visibility allows, offering a unique view of the Rock. You can yacht charter Puerto Banus for your chosen dates.

Which boat to rent?

  1. Rigid inflatable boat (RIB), best for small groups who want speed and agility. Up to 8 guests, from €250 per hour.
  2. Sleek motorboat (Axopar 28), ideal for a stylish couples' cruise; check the Limpopo listing for this well-equipped boat. Up to 10 guests, from €100 per hour.
  3. Catamaran (Lagoon 46), stable and comfortable for families and larger parties. Up to 12 guests, from €800 for a half-day.
  4. Crewed superyacht (Maiora 26), the ultimate luxury sunset experience with a captain and hostess. Up to 15 guests, from €2,500 for three hours.

How much does it cost to rent a boat in Puerto Banus?

Prices vary by vessel size, season and duration, but expect to pay between €350 and €3,000 for a private sunset cruise.

  • Shared sunset tour: from €60 per person, usually a two-hour catamaran sail.
  • Small motorboat (6, 8 metres) half-day: €300, €550 including fuel and skipper.
  • Catamaran full-day (up to 12 guests): €1,200, €2,000 with crew and refreshments.
  • Crewed luxury motor yacht (15+ metres) for sunset: €2,500, €4,000 including canapés and champagne.

Book early for the best selection; many well-maintained boats, such as those listed through a reliable platform like yacht charter Puerto Banus, get reserved weeks in advance during peak summer. One option worth a look is the Limpopo listing.

Do you need a licence to rent a boat in Puerto Banus?

In mainland Spain, a recreational boating licence (PER) is required for boats over 5 metres in length, but most hire boats include a professional skipper.

Tip: Even if you have a licence, opt for a local captain. He will know the best sunset anchorage spots around the Marbella coastline and can navigate the busy Marina Puerto Banus entrance without stress.

What is the best season for a sunset cruise in Puerto Banus?

The high season runs from June to September, when the weather is reliably warm and the famous Poniente wind, a light westerly breeze, often picks up in the late afternoon, making for a pleasant sailing experience. Spring (April, May) and autumn (October) offer lower prices and fewer crowds, though the sunset is still spectacular. Winter trips are possible but subject to weather; operators may cancel if the wind (especially the Levante) exceeds safe limits.

At a glance

  • Price range: €350, €4,000 per private sunset cruise
  • Peak months: June, July, August, September
  • Trip length: 2, 4 hours for sunset, half-day or full-day available
  • Max capacity: typically 8, 15 guests, depending on boat type
  • Currency: EUR

Questions about boat rental in Puerto Banus

Can I charter a boat without a skipper in Puerto Banus?

Yes, if you hold a valid Spanish PER (Patrón de Embarcaciones de Recreo) or an equivalent international licence, you can rent a bareboat vessel from some local companies.

What is included in the price of a sunset cruise?

Most listings include fuel, a qualified skipper, and basic insurance; extras such as food, drinks, and marina fees are usually negotiated separately.

How many people can join a sunset cruise on a 28ft boat?

A 28ft boat like the Axopar 28 comfortably accommodates up to 10 passengers, but the legal maximum is determined by the boat's capacity plate and the operator's insurance.

Can I bring my own champagne on board?

Most operators allow you to bring your own food and drinks, though some may charge a corkage or cleaning fee, always check with the owner before booking.

Planning a sunset charter from Puerto Banus is straightforward with a trusted aggregator like BoatHire24, where you can browse real photos, read reviews, and book directly. If you own a boat at this marina or elsewhere on the Costa del Sol, consider listing it on BoatHire24 to reach thousands of travellers seeking the perfect evening on the water.

Written by the BoatHire24 team, who book and list verified boats in Puerto Banus and hundreds of destinations worldwide.
